ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of sensor (e.g., temperature, exhaust vacuum) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the sensor (e.g., Celsius, Hg) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the sensor is installed */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the sensor was installed */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration */,
  "status" TEXT /* Operational status of the sensor (e.g., active, maintenance, faulty) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "readings" (
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reading */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that took the reading */,
  "reading_value" REAL /* Value of the reading */,
  "reading_time" TEXT /* Time when the reading was taken */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the reading was taken */,
  "season" TEXT /* Season when the reading was taken (e.g., spring, summer, fall, winter) */,
  "time_of_day" TEXT /* Time of day when the reading was taken (e.g., morning, afternoon, evening, night) */,
  "weather_conditions" TEXT /* Weather conditions during the reading (e.g., sunny, rainy, foggy) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_readings_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "temperature_readings" (
  "temperature_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each temperature reading */,
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reading */,
  "temperature_celsius" REAL /* Temperature reading in Celsius */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("temperature_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_temperature_readings_reading_id FOREIGN KEY ("reading_id") REFERENCES readings ("reading_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "exhaust_vacuum_readings" (
  "exhaust_vacuum_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each exhaust vacuum reading */,
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reading */,
  "exhaust_vacuum_hg" REAL /* Exhaust vacuum reading in Hg */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("exhaust_vacuum_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exhaust_vacuum_readings_reading_id FOREIGN KEY ("reading_id") REFERENCES readings ("reading_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ambient_pressure_readings" (
  "ambient_pressure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ambient pressure reading */,
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reading */,
  "ambient_pressure_millibar" REAL /* Ambient pressure reading in millibar */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ambient_pressure_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ambient_pressure_readings_reading_id FOREIGN KEY ("reading_id") REFERENCES readings ("reading_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "relative_humidity_readings" (
  "relative_humidity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relative humidity reading */,
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reading */,
  "relative_humidity" REAL /* Relative humidity reading */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relative_humidity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_relative_humidity_readings_reading_id FOREIGN KEY ("reading_id") REFERENCES readings ("reading_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hourly_energy_output_readings" (
  "hourly_energy_output_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hourly energy output reading */,
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reading */,
  "hourly_energy_output_mw" REAL /* Hourly energy output reading in MW */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hourly_energy_output_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hourly_energy_output_readings_reading_id FOREIGN KEY ("reading_id") REFERENCES readings ("reading_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "equipment" (
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each equipment */,
  "equipment_name" TEXT /* Name of the equipment */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of equipment (e.g., turbine, generator) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the equipment */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the equipment was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance_due" TEXT /* Date when the next maintenance is due */,
  "status" TEXT /* Operational status of the equipment (e.g., operational, under maintenance, faulty) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("equipment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_schedules" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance schedule */,
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the equipment */,
  "schedule_date" TEXT /* Date of the maintenance schedule */,
  "schedule_time" TEXT /* Time of the maintenance schedule */,
  "completed" INTEGER /* Whether the maintenance has been completed */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any notes or comments about the maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_schedules_equipment_id FOREIGN KEY ("equipment_id") REFERENCES equipment ("equipment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., operator, maintenance, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time of access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "device" TEXT /* Device used for access (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "equipment_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance data */,
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the equipment */,
  "performance_date" TEXT /* Date of the performance data */,
  "performance_value" REAL /* Value of the performance data */,
  "performance_metric" TEXT /* Performance metric being measured (e.g., efficiency, output) */,
  "measurement_unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the performance value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_equipment_performance_equipment_id FOREIGN KEY ("equipment_id") REFERENCES equipment ("equipment_id")
)
